gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 review found no blocking issues. The reviewer confirmed the live_server handoff path, request_id and payload_uri handling, expected_backend validation, public export/stub, and README language are consistent with moving toward live SGLang decode-time prefix-binding evidence without adding a solver or overclaiming benchmark evidence. GitHub would not allow the reviewer process to approve because it authenticated as the PR author, so the clean result was posted as a review comment.
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_live_server.py tests/test_public_package.py -q -> 28 passed
poetry check && poetry check --lock -> All set! / All set!
poetry run python -m document_kv_cache.live_server --help shows --handoff-json, --handoff-record-json, --payload-uri, --request-id, and --expected-backend
GitHub CI Test and build passed on initial PR head 6d2c7a45b918e2532f69ccaf5865eb9a77dc100d
GPT-5.5 reviewer found no blocking issues and posted a COMMENTED review because GitHub rejected self-approval by the PR author identity
what_changed: Added strict live-check handoff parameter construction from validated engine-adapter handoff JSON or inline handoff records.
Attached Cachet kv_transfer_params and request_id to cache-arm OpenAI-compatible live endpoint checks.
Documented the SGLang live validation command and updated the Cachet public export and typing stub.
why: The existing live endpoint smoke could label a request as cache-arm but could not send Cachet handoff metadata. This moves SGLang validation toward a real live decode-time prefix-binding run without adding a custom solver or claiming new benchmark evidence.
